text:
Once upon a time in a quaint little town by the sea, there lived a writer. Every evening after supper, she'd retreat to her cluttered writing desk, surrounded by ancient manuscript notebooks and piles of forgotten ideas.

There was one story she always dreamed of telling—one filled with adventure, friendship, and brave birds. She would sit for hours, dreaming up every detail, crafting each sentence to perfection.

One day, after countless nights filled with typing and erasing, the story was complete. It was about a family of magnificent scarlet birds who traveled across continents, seeking treasures lost in ancient forests and singing legends into the stars.

With trembling fingers, she typed out the opening lines. It began like this:

"In the heart of an old, whispering forest stood an ancient oak...

..."

Her editor loved it. Critics called it a heartwarming adventure—a true masterpiece. People everywhere wanted it printed. Our scarlet heroine became a beloved heroine.

Our little heroine, named Seraphina,
